title: fix(release): remove preview diff layout assumptions
summary: This is a clean, well-motivated refactor. The core fix â€” replacing the hardcoded `packages/<scope>` path matching with real package-directory resolution â€” is correct and properly tested. The extraction of diff loading into `pr-preview-diff.ts` is well-structured. Three minor issues are worth flagging.

findings.title: Schema.encodeUnknownEffect / encodeUnknownSync are unverified in Effect beta.31
findings.body:
The PR replaces `Schema.encode` / `Schema.encodeSync` with `Schema.encodeUnknownEffect` / `Schema.encodeUnknownSync`. The `decode`-side renames (`decodeUnknownEffect`, `decodeUnknownSync`) are confirmed throughout the codebase. However, `encodeUnknownEffect` and `encodeUnknownSync` appear in no other file in the repo â€” they cannot be cross-validated from local usage. The official Effect v4 docs (effect.website) only list `encode` (returns Effect) and `encodeSync` as the encode variants.

If beta.31 does not export `encodeUnknownEffect` / `encodeUnknownSync`, these static methods will be `undefined` at runtime (or TypeScript will error at build time). The `bun run release:verify` step in the PR description should catch this, but worth confirming the CI gate actually ran successfully before assuming these API names are valid.

findings.title: Unused Diff type import in pr-preview-diff.ts
findings.body:
Line 8 imports both `ChangedFile` and `Diff` from `'../api/lint/services/diff.js'`, but `Diff` is never referenced in the file â€” only `ChangedFile` is used. This is a dead import that will trigger any unused-import lint rule.

```ts
// Before
import type { ChangedFile, Diff } from '../api/lint/services/diff.js'
// After
import type { ChangedFile } from '../api/lint/services/diff.js'
```

findings.title: resolveDiffRemote called twice in the doctor command
findings.body:
`resolveDiffRemote(config)` is computed explicitly and stored in `diffRemote` (used for the `env.git-remote` rule option), but then `loadConfiguredPullRequestDiff({ config, ... })` internally calls `resolveDiffRemote(config)` a second time from the same config.

This is not a correctness bug â€” both reads produce the same value. But the doctor site could instead call `loadPullRequestDiff({ ..., remote: diffRemote })` directly to make the data flow explicit and avoid the redundant computation:

```ts
const diffRemote = resolveDiffRemote(config)
const diff = pullRequest
  ? yield* loadPullRequestDiff({ pullRequest, packages, required: false, remote: diffRemote })
  : null
```

residual_risks: The test mock uses `as any` for the `stdin` and `getInputFd` fields of the ChildProcessHandle. If the handle interface changes in a future Effect beta, these mock fields could silently mismatch without a type error.
DiffRemoteConfigLike uses Record<string, { options?: Record<string, unknown> }> for rules, so a typo in 'env.git-remote' anywhere that uses resolveDiffRemote would not be caught at compile time.
